info.title: Confirmation of Availability of Funds
info.description:
This service allows to confirm the availability of funds before initiating a payment. This service enables to verify that a payment can be successfully completed and avoids the possibility of insufficient funds or payment declines. Also, it provides a real-time response indicating the availability of funds and can also include additional details such as the account balance and credit limit.

![JOF1](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2023-12/jpc-internal-jof-banner.png)

---

**_This is the initial draft of the Jordan Open Finance specification_**

The services in this Open API specification are Direct-Call Services (DCS). These services can be accessed by direct calls to the banks or by using an aggregator central hub such as JoPACC. However, the URLs and routes for these services are designed for direct-calls only.

- For the Open API specification of the aggregated services, refer to the Aggregator Services specification.

Services in Jordan Open Finance Specification (JOF) are divided into 4 categories based on their content and nature of consent.

# **Account Information Services (AIS)**

This category covers services that are related to customers' financial accounts.This category has two sub-categories:

1. **MY AIS**: Services that allow an account holder to view their own information. For a TPP to retrieve this information, they require the consent of the user.

2. **Others AIS**: Services that enable a TPP to view the account information of a user who is not their customer and who hasn't initiated the request. These services require the user whose information is being retrieved to have consented, such as the IBAN Confirmation service.

The Confirmation of Availability of Funds service is one of the AIS services and it provides detailed information about the availability of funds in a specific account.

### **Overview**

---

The **Confirmation of Availability of Funds** service lets you check if there are enough funds available before making a payment. It helps ensure that payments go through smoothly and avoids issues like insufficient funds or declined payments. Additionally, it provides instant feedback on fund availability and may also include extra information like account balance and credit limit.

#### **Confirmation of Availability of Funds Service**

**1. Create CAF request**

### **Endpoint**

---

### **POST /accounts/{accountId}/CAF**

#### **Create a funds confirmation request:** With this service, you can request confirmation of funds for a specific account by providing its account ID

|     | Resource | Method | EndPoint                  |
| --- | -------- | ------ | ------------------------- |
| 1   | CAF      | GET    | /accounts/{accountId}/CAF |

### **Parameters**

---

The table below outlines the inputs or configurations required for invoking the service.

|     | ParameterName | Required/Optional | Description            |
| --- | ------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| 1   | accountId     | Required          | Account id of the user |

### **Headers**

---

The table below lists the HTTP headers that can be included in requests to provide additional information or configuration.

|     | HeaderName            | Required/Optional | Description                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --- | --------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | Authorization         | Required          | This header typically carries credentials, such as tokens or keys, allowing the server to authenticate the client's request and determine whether it has permission to access the requested resource. |
| 2   | x-interactions-id     | Required          | This header is not a standard HTTP header, but it is used as a unique id for the interaction, used in all requests and responses that are related                                                     |
| 3   | x-idempotency-key     | Required          | A unique identifier used to ensure idempotent API requests, preventing duplicate actions when the same request is made multiple times.                                                                |
| 4   | x-financial-id        | Optional          | A custom header potentially used in financial APIs to convey specific transaction or account identifiers.                                                                                             |
| 5   | x-jws-signature       | Required          | A custom header indicating the presence of a JSON Web Signature (JWS) for verifying the authenticity and integrity of API requests.                                                                   |
| 6   | x-auth-date           | Optional          | A custom header used to indicate the date and time of authentication in API requests for security and audit trail purposes.                                                                           |
| 7   | x-customer-id         | Optional          | A custom header containing the unique identifier of the user associated with the API request.                                                                                                         |
| 8   | x-customer-ip-address | Optional          | A custom header providing the IP address of the user making the API request for tracking or authentication purposes.                                                                                  |
| 9   | x-customer-user-agent | Optional          | A custom header indicating the user-agent string of the customer's browser or client application making the API request.                                                                              |

## **UML Diagram**

---

#### **Request Body**

![CAFUMLREQUEST](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2024-05/confirmation_of_availability_of_funds_-_request_qxjjdp.png)

#### **Response**

![CAFUMLRESPONSE](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2024-05/confirmation_of_availability_of_funds_-_response_dnhmyz.png)

### **Data Dictionary**

---

####

#### **POST Service - Request Body and Response**

The table below represents the API service fields and serves as a comprehensive reference detailing the meaning, format, and usage of each field, ensuring a consistent understanding of the service.

| #   | **RequetBody fields**   | Data Type           | JSON Path                         | Description                                      |
| --- | ----------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| 1   |  postCAF            | N/A                 | N/A                               | Method to create a funds confirmation request    |
| 2   | Request Body            | N/A                 | N/A                               | The request body of the HTTP request             |
| 3   | instructionAmount       | object(ComplexType) | instructionAmount                 | Amount and currency for the instruction          |
| #   | **Response**            | Data Type           | JSON Path                         | Description                                      |
| 1   |  postCAF               | N/A                 | N/A                               | Method to create a funds confirmation request    |
| 2   | Response                | N/A                 | N/A                               | The response from the HTTP request               |
| 3   | validityDateTime        | string              | validityDateTime                  | Date and time indicating validity                |
| 4   | fundsAvailable          | boolean             | fundsAvailable                    | Indicator for funds availability                 |
| 5   | instructionAmount       | object(ComplexType) | instructionAmount                 | Amount and currency for the instruction          |

### **Complex Types Dictionary**

---

The tables below represent a structured overview of composite data structures utilized within the API, detailing their constituent attributes and relationships.

#### **amountComplexType**

| Info.           |                                                                   |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Description** | A complex type containing basic information about an institution. |
| **Used in**     | instructionAmount                                                 |
| **JSON Path**   | instructionAmount                                                 |

#####

##### **_amountComplexType in details_**

| \*  | **Field** | Data Type | **JSON Path**              | **Description**                  |
| --- | --------- | --------- | -------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| 1   | amount    | number    | instructionAmount.amount   | The amount of the instruction.   |
| 2   | currency  | enum      | instructionAmount.currency | The currency of the instruction. |

The Central Bank of Jordan has classified Open Finance consent into Account Information consent and Payment Initiation consent. The above categories are based on the CBJ's classifications and divide them further for the user's convenience.

Some useful links:

- [About JoPACC](https://jopacc.com)

parameters.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

parameters.description: Date and time of the last authentication of the User by the TPP

parameters.description: The User's IP address if the user is currently logged in with the TPP

parameters.name: x-customer-user-agent
parameters.description: Indicates the user-agent the User is using. Helps protecting from comprimised devices
